# Skill: sense-explore

# Explore codebase with Sense

Use Sense MCP tools to navigate the codebase structurally.

## Steps

1. Run `sense_status` to confirm the index is healthy.
2. Run `sense_search query="<topic>"` to find relevant symbols.
3. For each interesting symbol, run `sense_graph symbol="<name>"` to see callers, callees, and relationships.
4. If a symbol has many connections, run `sense_graph symbol="<name>" direction="callers"` or `sense_graph symbol="<name>" direction="callees"` to focus.
5. Summarize the architecture you found: key symbols, how they connect, entry points.

3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Anthropic class implements a provider for
6:   # [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com).
7:   #
8:   # @example
9:   #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
10:   #   require "llm"
11:   #
12:   #   llm = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["KEY"])
13:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
14:   #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this photo", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
15:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
16:   class Anthropic < Provider
17:     require_relative "anthropic/utils"
18:     require_relative "anthropic/error_handler"
19:     require_relative "anthropic/request_adapter"
20:     require_relative "anthropic/response_adapter"
21:     require_relative "anthropic/stream_parser"
22:     require_relative "anthropic/models"
23:     require_relative "anthropic/files"
24:     include RequestAdapter
25:     extend Utils
26: 
27:     HOST = "api.anthropic.com"
28: 
29:     ##
30:     # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
31:     def initialize(**)
32:       super(host: HOST, **)
33:     end
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @return [Symbol]
37:     #  Returns the provider's name
38:     def name
39:       :anthropic
40:     end
41: 
42:     ##
43:     # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
44:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/messages Anthropic docs
45:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
46:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
47:     # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
48:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
49:     # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
50:     #  When given an object a provider does not understand
51:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
52:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
53:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
54:       req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
55:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
56:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
57:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
58:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
59:       res
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # Provides an interface to Anthropic's models API
64:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/models-list
65:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::Models]
66:     def models
67:       LLM::Anthropic::Models.new(self)
68:     end
69: 
70:     ##
71:     # Provides an interface to Anthropic's files API
72:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/files Anthropic docs
73:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::Files]
74:     def files
75:       LLM::Anthropic::Files.new(self)
76:     end
77: 
78:     ##
79:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
80:     def assistant_role
81:       "assistant"
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # Anthropic expects tool results to be sent as user messages
86:     # containing `tool_result` content blocks rather than a distinct
87:     # `tool` role.
88:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#tool_role)
89:     def tool_role
90:       :user
91:     end
92: 
93:     ##
94:     # Returns the default model for chat completions
95:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models/all-models#model-comparison-table claude-sonnet-4-20250514
96:     # @return [String]
97:     def default_model
98:       "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
99:     end
100: 
101:     ##
102:     # @note
103:     #  This method includes certain tools that require configuration
104:     #  through a set of options that are easier to set through the
105:     #  {LLM::Provider#server_tool LLM::Provider#server_tool} method.
106:     # @see https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents-and-tools/tool-use/web-search-tool Anthropic docs
107:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#server_tools)
108:     def server_tools
109:       {
110:         bash: server_tool(:bash, type: "bash_20250124"),
111:         web_search: server_tool(:web_search, type: "web_search_20250305", max_uses: 5),
112:         text_editor: server_tool(:str_replace_based_edit_tool, type: "text_editor_20250728", max_characters: 10_000)
113:       }
114:     end
115: 
116:     ##
117:     # A convenience method for performing a web search using the
118:     # Anthropic web search tool.
119:     # @example
120:     #   llm = LLM.anthropic(key: ENV["KEY"])
121:     #   res = llm.web_search(query: "summarize today's news")
122:     #   res.search_results.each { |item| print item.title, ": ", item.url, "\n" }
123:     # @param query [String] The search query.
124:     # @return [LLM::Response] The response from the LLM provider.
125:     def web_search(query:)
126:       ResponseAdapter.adapt(complete(query, tools: [server_tools[:web_search]]), type: :web_search)
127:     end
128: 
129:     private
130: 
131:     def headers
132:       lock do
133:         (@headers || {}).merge(
134:           "Content-Type" => "application/json",
135:           "x-api-key" => @key,
136:           "anthropic-version" => "2023-06-01",
137:           "anthropic-beta" => "files-api-2025-04-14"
138:         )
139:       end
140:     end
141: 
142:     def stream_parser
143:       LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser
144:     end
145: 
146:     def error_handler
147:       LLM::Anthropic::ErrorHandler
148:     end
149: 
150:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
151:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 1024}.merge!(params)
152:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
153:       params = [params, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
154:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
155:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
156:       [params, stream, tools, role]
157:     end
158: 
159:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
160:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
161:       payload = adapt(messages)
162:       body = LLM.json.dump(payload.merge!(params))
163:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/messages", headers)
164:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
165:       req
166:     end
167: 
168:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
169:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
170:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
171:       else
172:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
173:       end
174:     end
175:   end
176: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::Anthropic::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the Anthropic chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
21:         else
22:           adapt_message
23:         end
24:       end
25:     end
26: 
27:     private
28: 
29:     def adapt_message
30:       if message.tool_call?
31:         {role: message.role, content: adapt_tool_calls}
32:       else
33:         {role: message.role, content: adapt_content(content)}
34:       end
35:     end
36: 
37:     def adapt_tool_calls
38:       message.extra[:tool_calls].filter_map do |tool|
39:         next unless tool[:id] && tool[:name]
40:         {type: "tool_use", id: tool[:id], name: tool[:name], input: LLM::Anthropic.parse_tool_input(tool[:arguments])}
41:       end
42:     end
43: 
44:     ##
45:     # @param [String, URI] content
46:     #  The content to format
47:     # @return [String, Hash]
48:     #  The formatted content
49:     def adapt_content(content)
50:       case content
51:       when Hash
52:         content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : [content]
53:       when Array
54:         content.empty? ? throw(:abort, nil) : content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }
55:       when LLM::Object
56:         adapt_object(content)
57:       when String
58:         [{type: :text, text: content}]
59:       when LLM::Response
60:         adapt_remote_file(content)
61:       when LLM::Message
62:         adapt_content(content.content)
63:       when LLM::Function::Return
64:         [{type: "tool_result", tool_use_id: content.id, content: [{type: :text, text: LLM.json.dump(content.value)}]}]
65:       else
66:         prompt_error!(content)
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     def adapt_object(object)
71:       case object.kind
72:       when :image_url
73:         [{type: :image, source: {type: "url", url: object.value.to_s}}]
74:       when :local_file
75:         adapt_local_file(object.value)
76:       when :remote_file
77:         adapt_remote_file(object.value)
78:       else
79:         prompt_error!(content)
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def adapt_local_file(file)
84:       if file.image?
85:         [{type: :image, source: {type: "base64", media_type: file.mime_type, data: file.to_b64}}]
86:       elsif file.pdf?
87:         [{type: :document, source: {type: "base64", media_type: file.mime_type, data: file.to_b64}}]
88:       else
89:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{file.class}) " \
90:                                 "is not an image or PDF, and therefore not supported by the " \
91:                                 "Anthropic API"
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     def adapt_remote_file(file)
96:       prompt_error!(file) unless file.file?
97:       [{type: file.file_type, source: {type: :file, file_id: file.id}}]
98:     end
99: 
100:     def prompt_error!(content)
101:       if LLM::Object === content
102:       else
103:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{content.class}) " \
104:                                 "is not supported by the Anthropic API."
105:       end
106:     end
107: 
108:     def message = @message
109:     def content = message.content
110:   end
111: end

7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
14:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
15:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {"role" => "assistant", "content" => []}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
20:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
21:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
22:     end
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # @param [Hash] chunk
26:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
27:     def parse!(chunk)
28:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
33:     # @return [void]
34:     def free
35:     end
36: 
37:     private
38: 
39:     def merge!(chunk)
40:       if chunk["type"] == "message_start"
41:         merge_message!(chunk["message"])
42:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_start"
43:         @body["content"][chunk["index"]] = chunk["content_block"]
44:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_delta"
45:         if chunk["delta"]["type"] == "text_delta"
46:           @body["content"][chunk["index"]]["text"] << chunk["delta"]["text"]
47:           emit_content(chunk["delta"]["text"])
48:         elsif chunk["delta"]["type"] == "input_json_delta"
49:           content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
50:           if Hash === content["input"]
51:             content["input"] = chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
52:           else
53:             content["input"] ||= +""
54:             content["input"] << chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
55:           end
56:         end
57:       elsif chunk["type"] == "message_delta"
58:         merge_message!(chunk["delta"]) if chunk["delta"]
59:         extras = chunk.reject { |k, _| k == "type" || k == "delta" }
60:         merge_message!(extras) unless extras.empty?
61:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_stop"
62:         content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
63:         if content["input"]
64:           content["input"] = LLM.json.load(content["input"])
65:         end
66:         if content["type"] == "tool_use"
67:           emit_tool(content)
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def merge_message!(message)
73:       message.each_pair do |key, value|
74:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair)
75:           @body[key] ||= {}
76:           deep_merge!(@body[key], value)
77:         else
78:           @body[key] = value
79:         end
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def deep_merge!(target, source)
84:       source.each_pair do |key, value|
85:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair) && target[key].respond_to?(:each_pair)
86:           deep_merge!(target[key], value)
87:         else
88:           target[key] = value
89:         end
90:       end
91:     end
92: 
93:     def emit_content(value)
94:       if @can_emit_content
95:         @stream.on_content(value)
96:       elsif @can_push_content
97:         @stream << value
98:       end
99:     end
100: 
101:     def emit_tool(tool)
102:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
103:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool)
104:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
105:     end
106: 
107:     def resolve_tool(tool)
108:       registered = @stream.__find__(tool["name"])
109:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(tool["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
110:         fn.id = tool["id"]
111:         fn.arguments = LLM::Anthropic.parse_tool_input(tool["input"])
112:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
113:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
114:       end
115:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
116:     end
117:   end

25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span

## Step 1: Codebase Orientation

This is the llm.rb library - a Ruby gem for interacting with various LLM providers. The code is organized as:

- **Entry point**: `lib/llm.rb` - defines the main `LLM` module with provider factory methods
- **Base provider**: `lib/llm/provider.rb` - abstract base class that all providers inherit from
- **Provider implementations**: `lib/llm/providers/` - contains directories for each provider (anthropic, google, openai, etc.)
- **Shared machinery**: `lib/llm/` - contains shared components like transport, context, messages, etc.

## Step 2: Chat Call Flow

A chat call flows through these layers:
1. `LLM.anthropic(**)` in `lib/llm.rb:112-115` - creates provider instance
2. `LLM::Context.new(provider, params).talk(prompt, role:)` in `lib/llm/provider.rb:105`
3. `provider.complete(prompt, params)` in `lib/llm/providers/anthropic.rb:52-60`
4. Request building via `build_complete_request` and `RequestAdapter.adapt` in `lib/llm/providers/anthropic/request_adapter.rb:13-26`
5. Response parsing via `ResponseAdapter.adapt` in `lib/llm/providers/anthropic/response_adapter/completion.rb:5-124`

## Step 3: Provider Contract

The shared contract in `lib/llm/provider.rb` requires:
- `name` - provider identifier
- `complete(prompt, params)` - main completion method
- `headers` - authentication headers
- `stream_parser` - streaming response parser class
- `error_handler` - error handling class
- `assistant_role`, `default_model`, `server_tools` - provider-specific defaults

## Step 4: Provider Components

Each provider has parallel components:
- **Request building**: `request_adapter.rb` with `adapt` method
- **Response parsing**: `response_adapter/completion.rb` with contract methods
- **Stream parsing**: `stream_parser.rb` with `parse!` method
- **Error handling**: `error_handler.rb` with `raise_error!` method
- **Additional APIs**: `models.rb`, `files.rb`, etc. as needed

## Step 5: Streaming and Error Handling

- **Streaming**: Each provider's `stream_parser` class handles incremental parsing
- **Errors**: Each provider's `error_handler` maps HTTP errors to `LLM::Error` subclasses

## Step 6: Required Changes for New Provider

To add a new provider, these files need changes:
- `lib/llm.rb` - add factory method
- `lib/llm/providers/` - create new provider directory with all components
- Test files in `spec/` - add provider-specific tests
